insights:
The single best deal is inclusionAI's Ling-2.6-flash, which tops the composite deal score at 840.0. Its intelligence score of only 14 out of 60 reflects limited reasoning ability, but the model delivers a staggering 123 tokens per second with a 262K context window, all at a price point that makes it nearly free for high-volume inference. For any production pipeline where the bulk of requests involve trivial classification, formatting, or extraction work—tasks that do not require deep understanding—this model offers the lowest cost per completion by a wide margin, sacrificing capability for unmatched throughput.

The most compelling runners-up balance intelligence and cost. DeepSeek V4 Flash earns a deal score of 306.1, pairing an intelligence score of 40 with a speed of 120 t/s and an enormous 1,000K context window, making it the strongest all-purpose bargain for anything that requires real comprehension. Tencent’s Hy3 leads the intelligence ranking among the top value picks at 41, with a deal score of 143.0, 63 t/s, and 256K context—its trade-off is slower generation and a lower overall value score, but it wins on raw capability per dollar. MiMo-V2.5 (intelligence 37, code ELO 1429, 1,000K context) scores 198.2 in value, offering excellent coding performance and large context at a very low input price of $0.14 per million tokens, though its 66 t/s is the slowest in this group.

For the most common cost-sensitive API use—running a mix of simple and moderately complex queries in production—I recommend DeepSeek V4 Flash as the default. It delivers sufficient intelligence for most tasks (40/60), provides a 1,000K context for long documents, and achieves 120 t/s at a value score that is second only to the extremely low-intelligence Ling-2.6-flash. Reserve Ling-2.6-flash for the highest-volume, lowest-complexity tier of requests, and fall back to DeepSeek V4 Flash for everything else to keep costs minimal without sacrificing usable quality.
